I’m not so certain our system checks the sendgrid api key for validity rather than just accepting any input and displaying’ connected’. We need to check the api key for access to the account. Also the ‘load my lists’ button is rejected as 401

I just disconnected and reconnected sendgrid with a fresh api key to see if the connected user and contact lists would populate and they’re not, so we’ve not included some back end info that’s necessary for these to function.

To clear up some of the trouble we have had with choosing audience/contact list id’s, we can try to use functions that create new lists based on custom data. For example, for sendgrid, wee can ‘Add Contacts to a new list’ and for every user have the new list be ‘ListingBug Contacts’. For mailchimp we can have a default tag added ‘ListingBug Contacts’. For hubspot, it appears a contact can be added to the account and doesn’t need any specific list, however we should find a way to differentiate (perhaps ‘Web analytics history: Original Traffic Source: ListingBug’)

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
